perturbation.original_text: A. Product. Shi Farms agrees to sell Product and Gridiron agrees to purchase 30,000 lbs. of hemp biomass ("Biomass") from Shi Farms. Biomass must contain a minimum of six percent (6%) total Cannabidiol (CBD/and or CBDA) and all Biomass must have less than three percent (3%) total TCH content. The Biomass must contain no contaminates that are above acceptable industry standards for processing Biomass including but not limited to: Mold and Mildew; Non- Hemp Plant Material; Soil; Insects; Rodent Droppings; Wet or Rotting Material; Heavy Metals; Residual Pesticides or Herbicides; Bacteria.
perturbation.changed_text: A. Product. Shi Farms agrees to sell Product and Gridiron agrees to purchase hemp biomass ("Biomass") from Shi Farms. The Biomass must contain no contaminates that are above acceptable industry standards for processing Biomass including but not limited to: Mold and Mildew; Non- Hemp Plant Material; Soil; Insects; Rodent Droppings; Wet or Rotting Material; Heavy Metals; Residual Pesticides or Herbicides; Bacteria.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific quantity and CBD/THC content requirements for the biomass, the modified text creates an internal contradiction. The original clause defined clear specifications (30,000 lbs., specific CBD/THC levels), while later sections assume these specifications exist. Removing these specifications creates ambiguity about the quality and quantity of biomass required under this agreement and how Gridiron may be protected if the biomass does not contain any cannabidiol (CBD/and or CBDA) or if the level is too low to be acceptable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. Pricing. Both Parties mutually agree that the Purchase Price of the Product is determined on a $5.00 per pound basis for a total cost of one hundred fifty thousand dollars ($150,000).
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a quantity of 30,000 lbs of biomass and minimum CBD/THC content. The changed text removes these specifications. The contradicted text (B. Pricing) sets the purchase price based on a 'per pound basis for a total cost of one hundred fifty thousand dollars ($150,000)'. Without the quantity specification in the changed text, the pricing clause becomes contradictory as it is unclear what quantity the $5.00 per pound and $150,000 total cost are based on, creating an inconsistency regarding the obligations of each party. The removal breaks the compatibility of Section A and Section B, therefore, the changed text contradicts the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: B. Pricing. Both Parties mutually agree that the Purchase Price of the Product is determined on a $5.00 per pound basis for a total cost of one hundred fifty thousand dollars ($150,000).
perturbation.changed_text: B. Pricing. Both Parties mutually agree that the Purchase Price of the Product will be determined at a later date.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the pricing terms to be determined at a later date, but leaving in the original text for the payment terms, this creates an internal contradiction because one of the conditions for payment to occur is not in place, which is the price for the biomass. The contract text now references a payment based on a price per pound, but fails to define the price per pound.
perturbation.contradicted_text: D. Payment. Both Parties agree that once the Parties have agreed to transaction which includes the purchase and sale of the Product that the Buyer will remit payment upon execution of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific price per pound, stating that the price will be determined at a later date. The contradicted text states that payment will be remitted upon execution of the agreement once the parties have agreed to a transaction which includes the purchase and sale of the product. Because the price has not been determined, there is no agreement as to the purchase and sale of the product, so the payment terms cannot be determined, thus creating a direct contradiction. The original text specified a price, which facilitated the payment upon execution clause. This aligns with the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: A. Termination. Either Party may terminate this Agreement at any time prior to delivery of the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: A. Termination. Either Party may terminate this Agreement at any time.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'prior to delivery of the Product' creates an internal contradiction within the text. The text references a delivery that is no longer in the contract and it is not defined, but it referenced in this section. The delivery is no longer specified, creating confusion about when termination can occur. Without that clause, it is unclear how far along the agreement needs to be completed for it to not be able to be terminated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: E. Quarantine. Once the payment has been received, Shi Farms will use its best efforts to quarantine product to ensure safe keeping of the Product until delivery date as agreed by the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'prior to delivery of the Product' in the changed text creates a contradiction with section E. Quarantine. The contradicted text relies on the concept of a 'delivery date as agreed by the Parties'. However, the changed termination clause now allows termination at any time without the restriction of 'prior to delivery', creating uncertainty and potential conflict regarding the quarantine obligations before delivery. The original text restricted termination 'prior to the delivery' date. Now, termination can occur after payment is received by Shi Farms, which would then contradict the Quarantine section.
